## [Quantum Mechanics May Describe Not Reality Itself, But the Mechanism of Its Emergence](https://habr.com/en/articles/1020096/)

Level of difficultyMedium

Reading time7 min

Reach and readers848

[Physics](https://habr.com/en/hubs/physics/)[Quantum technologies](https://habr.com/en/hubs/quantum/)[Start-up development](https://habr.com/en/hubs/startuprise/)[Systems engineering \\*](https://habr.com/en/hubs/engineering_systems/)[Popular science](https://habr.com/en/hubs/popular_science/)

Translation

![](https://habrastorage.org/r/w780/getpro/habr/upload_files/2aa/105/c15/2aa105c156c97c0a3b87377006fb4647.jpg)

Quantum mechanics is one of the most successful theories in the history of science.

It underlies atomic physics, semiconductors, lasers, and modern quantum technologies.

However, nearly a century after its development, a peculiar situation remains:

we can predict experimental results with remarkable precision, yet we still do not fully understand **what quantum theory actually represents.**

Does it describe physical reality “as it is,” or does it instead describe the structure of conditions under which observable facts become possible?

In this article, I propose the following hypothesis:

**quantum theory does not describe reality itself, but the conditions and mechanisms through which observable reality emerges.**

[Read more](https://habr.com/en/articles/1020096/)

Level of difficultyEasy

Reading time5 min

Reach and readers7.2K

[Xeovo VPN corporate blog](https://habr.com/en/companies/xeovo/articles/)[Legislation in IT](https://habr.com/en/hubs/business-laws/)

Opinion

![](https://habrastorage.org/r/w1560/getpro/habr/upload_files/562/d76/b23/562d76b23f613aba011b828b68d4a6d8.png)

The Art of Comeback of the European Bureaucracy

On November 26, Members of the European Parliament rejected the Chat Control Act in its current version, i.e, mandatory scanning of the user traffic before it gets encrypted. Lawmakers, however, will hardly give up: requirement for mandatory scanning shall be reconsidered every three years. Xeovo explains what is the story behind this law and why, in reality, there’s little justification for passing it.

## [PostgreSQL cost model: estimating the cost of flushing temp buffers to disk](https://habr.com/en/companies/postgrespro/articles/986418/)

Level of difficultyMedium

Reading time8 min

Reach and readers4.8K

[Postgres Professional corporate blog](https://habr.com/en/companies/postgrespro/articles/)[PostgreSQL \\*](https://habr.com/en/hubs/postgresql/)

Roadmap

Translation

![](https://habrastorage.org/r/w780/getpro/habr/upload_files/91c/6b7/7bb/91c6b77bb3f7ca945ef208b590a353b9.jpg)

Today I present results from the first step towards enabling temporary tables in PostgreSQL parallel query execution: benchmarking sequential writes and reads of temp buffers. I added functions to the PostgreSQL system catalog to measure buffer flush operations and ran a set of tests. For the planner, I estimated the cost coefficient of flushing a temporary table buffer page to disk relative to the existing DEFAULT\_SEQ\_PAGE\_COST. I also estimated the cost of a simple scan of temp buffers (a dry-run). Measurements show that sequential writes are about 30% slower than reads. Based on these results, I propose a cost formula. I also ran a limited “dry-run” style estimate and measured write speed when table blocks are distributed across memory pages randomly.

[Read more](https://habr.com/en/companies/postgrespro/articles/986418/)

## [LoRa Module (P2P Private Protocol) Parameters](https://habr.com/en/articles/1014044/)

Level of difficultyMedium

Reading time21 min

Reach and readers4.4K

[Wireless technologies \\*](https://habr.com/en/hubs/wireless/)[Programming microcontrollers \\*](https://habr.com/en/hubs/controllers/)[Manufacture and development of electronics \\*](https://habr.com/en/hubs/electronics/)[IOT](https://habr.com/en/hubs/internet_of_things/)[IT Infrastructure \\*](https://habr.com/en/hubs/it-infrastructure/)

FAQ

![](https://habrastorage.org/r/w1560/getpro/habr/upload_files/ae2/b4e/afc/ae2b4eafc9d7ee5e16d7dd86ca9704fa.png)

If you are using a LoRa module for point-to-point (P2P) communication or building your own private protocol, understanding and correctly configuring each parameter is the key to successful communication.

**After reading this article, you will learn:**

• How Should the LoRa Over-the-Air (PHY) Modulation and Packet Format Parameters Be Configured, and Why?

• The meaning of RF front-end parameters and how to tune them

• Chip-level control parameters: operating modes, clocks, power supply, calibration, and more

• A quick-reference "must-match" table and a list of common issues

_Note: This article covers only LoRa (P2P/Private Protocol) parameters. If you need information on LoRaWAN protocol-layer parameters (e.g., ADR, DevEUI, join procedures), please refer to "LoRaWAN Protocol Layer Parameters"._

Before starting configuration, verify that your device has selected PacketType = LoRa. The LR2021 supports multiple modulation types—including LoRa, LR-FHSS, FLRC, FSK/GFSK, OQPSK, and OOK. When using P2P LoRa communication, you must first switch to LoRa mode; all subsequent modulation and packet structure commands will then be interpreted in LoRa mode.

If you select the wrong PacketType (e.g., FLRC or GFSK mode), the chip will not interpret the SF, BW, and CR parameters in LoRa mode, The result is that the transmitted data cannot be decoded by the receiving end at all.

[Read more](https://habr.com/en/articles/1014044/)

## [What will enterprise databases look like in the age of AI](https://habr.com/en/companies/postgrespro/articles/985004/)

Level of difficultyEasy

Reading time5 min

Reach and readers7.2K

[Postgres Professional corporate blog](https://habr.com/en/companies/postgrespro/articles/)[PostgreSQL \\*](https://habr.com/en/hubs/postgresql/)[Oracle \\*](https://habr.com/en/hubs/oracle/)

Opinion

Translation

![](https://habrastorage.org/r/w1560/getpro/habr/upload_files/f9d/78b/42e/f9d78b42ea842454afdd3716ab456be5.png)

There’s a dangerous illusion that “vanilla” open source is a silver bullet for enterprise systems. A real-world stress test of recent years has shown the opposite: when familiar giants like Oracle step aside, plain PostgreSQL often turns into a pumpkin under true enterprise workloads.

Mark Rivkin, Head of technical consulting at Postgres Professional, shares his personal perspective on why teams end up reinventing the wheel — adding millions of lines of code to the core — and why the future belongs to converged database systems.

_Disclaimer: this article reflects the author’s independent expert opinion._

## [Why LeCun's World Model Won't Save AI](https://habr.com/en/articles/1010934/)

Level of difficultyEasy

Reading time19 min

Reach and readers5.9K

[Artificial Intelligence](https://habr.com/en/hubs/artificial_intelligence/)[Machine learning \\*](https://habr.com/en/hubs/machine_learning/)

Opinion

![](https://habrastorage.org/r/w780/getpro/habr/upload_files/8e8/bdd/efa/8e8bddefabcae7ffa2fc23d6ce42f281.jpg)

After the unexpected divorce between LeCun and Meta, there is a lot of talk that the dead-end in LLM progress will be overcome through the physics of the world. That is, having a neural network work with physical data from the surrounding environment will allow the model to acquire meaning and an understanding of its actions. LeCun has a foundational [paper](https://openreview.net/pdf?id=BZ5a1r-kVsf) that nobody is going to read. So, I'll summarize it as best I can. Essentially, the idea is that the current trajectory of LLM development is doomed. As long as they are predicting the next token, real understanding — the emergence of real meaning — is impossible. LeCun proposes training neural networks on physical world data, assuming that building a model of it will allow the system to discard details and focus on meaning.

I agree with LeCun that using world data will partially solve the data scarcity problem. But here I see a problem that engineers might not understand. A physical model of the world is actually much poorer than human knowledge. Newton described the entire infinite number of possible falls with a few lines of formulas. I doubt LeCun wants to spend billions of dollars on this wonderful deduction.

[Read more](https://habr.com/en/articles/1010934/)

Rating0

Add to bookmarks0

Opinion

Translation

![](https://habrastorage.org/r/w1560/getpro/habr/upload_files/87c/8af/0e2/87c8af0e2241832fff4b127d97544471.png)

In the IT crowd, it’s common to mock HR for their weird terminology and attempts to assess a “rich inner world” instead of clean code. But when a senior starts tearing juniors apart in code reviews to the point where they end up crying in the bathroom, nobody’s laughing anymore.

We pit two archetypes against each other: the “toxic genius” and the “human glue”. Which one is dead weight, and which one is the load-bearing structure of the project? You might not like the answer.

## [A Quick Encrypted Flash Drivers Security Analysis: Real Protection or a Marketing Ploy?](https://habr.com/en/companies/bastion/articles/1000476/)

Reading time22 min

Reach and readers6.3K

[Бастион corporate blog](https://habr.com/en/companies/bastion/articles/)[Information Security \\*](https://habr.com/en/hubs/infosecurity/)[Reverse engineering \\*](https://habr.com/en/hubs/reverse-engineering/)[Data storaging](https://habr.com/en/hubs/storages/)[Data recovery \\*](https://habr.com/en/hubs/data_recovery/)

![](https://habrastorage.org/r/w1560/getpro/habr/upload_files/2f7/288/ce3/2f7288ce34cd57cbaa4317daf9b06ae7.png)

Hey, Habr! Ivan Glinkin is here again, head of the hardware research group from the Bastion team.

"A flash drive with a combination lock," "a flash drive with hardware encryption," "an encrypted USB drive," and finally, the proper term — "Cryptographic Module". An encrypted USB flash drive goes by many names, but the core concept remains the same.

The purpose of such a device is to protect sensitive information from unauthorized access at both the software and hardware levels through encryption, anti-tampering mechanisms, and various other safeguards. But are these secure USB drives really as reliable as they're made out to be, or is it all just smoke and mirrors?

We decided to look past the marketing claims and conduct our own investigation, attempting to crack several of these devices using hardware reverse engineering. We attempted to extract data, identify the encryption algorithms used, physically open the drives, and read their memory chips.

The results were quite interesting. Read on for the details.

[Read more](https://habr.com/en/companies/bastion/articles/1000476/)

Quantum Mechanics May Describe Not Reality Itself, But the Mechanism of Its Emergence

Medium
7 min
848
PhysicsQuantum technologiesStart-up developmentSystems engineering * Popular science
Translation

Quantum mechanics is one of the most successful theories in the history of science.

It underlies atomic physics, semiconductors, lasers, and modern quantum technologies.

However, nearly a century after its development, a peculiar situation remains:

we can predict experimental results with remarkable precision, yet we still do not fully understand what quantum theory actually represents.

Does it describe physical reality “as it is,” or does it instead describe the structure of conditions under which observable facts become possible?

In this article, I propose the following hypothesis:

quantum theory does not describe reality itself, but the conditions and mechanisms through which observable reality emerges.

melanny20 Mar 26 at 05:47

PostgreSQL cost model: estimating the cost of flushing temp buffers to disk

Medium
8 min
4.8K
Postgres Professional corporate blogPostgreSQL *
Roadmap
Translation

Today I present results from the first step towards enabling temporary tables in PostgreSQL parallel query execution: benchmarking sequential writes and reads of temp buffers. I added functions to the PostgreSQL system catalog to measure buffer flush operations and ran a set of tests. For the planner, I estimated the cost coefficient of flushing a temporary table buffer page to disk relative to the existing DEFAULT_SEQ_PAGE_COST. I also estimated the cost of a simple scan of temp buffers (a dry-run). Measurements show that sequential writes are about 30% slower than reads. Based on these results, I propose a cost formula. I also ran a limited “dry-run” style estimate and measured write speed when table blocks are distributed across memory pages randomly.

annjiang Mar 23 at 23:20

LoRa Module (P2P Private Protocol) Parameters

Medium
21 min
4.4K
Wireless technologies * Programming microcontrollers * Manufacture and development of electronics * IOTIT Infrastructure *
FAQ

If you are using a LoRa module for point-to-point (P2P) communication or building your own private protocol, understanding and correctly configuring each parameter is the key to successful communication.

After reading this article, you will learn:

• How Should the LoRa Over-the-Air (PHY) Modulation and Packet Format Parameters Be Configured, and Why?

• The meaning of RF front-end parameters and how to tune them

• Chip-level control parameters: operating modes, clocks, power supply, calibration, and more

• A quick-reference "must-match" table and a list of common issues

Note: This article covers only LoRa (P2P/Private Protocol) parameters. If you need information on LoRaWAN protocol-layer parameters (e.g., ADR, DevEUI, join procedures), please refer to "LoRaWAN Protocol Layer Parameters".

Before starting configuration, verify that your device has selected PacketType = LoRa. The LR2021 supports multiple modulation types—including LoRa, LR-FHSS, FLRC, FSK/GFSK, OQPSK, and OOK. When using P2P LoRa communication, you must first switch to LoRa mode; all subsequent modulation and packet structure commands will then be interpreted in LoRa mode.

If you select the wrong PacketType (e.g., FLRC or GFSK mode), the chip will not interpret the SF, BW, and CR parameters in LoRa mode, The result is that the transmitted data cannot be decoded by the receiving end at all.

ICU: character conversion function optimization
The parameter standard_conforming_strings can no longer be disabled

Kamil_GR Mar 16 at 10:35

Why LeCun's World Model Won't Save AI

Easy
19 min
5.9K
Artificial IntelligenceMachine learning *
Opinion

After the unexpected divorce between LeCun and Meta, there is a lot of talk that the dead-end in LLM progress will be overcome through the physics of the world. That is, having a neural network work with physical data from the surrounding environment will allow the model to acquire meaning and an understanding of its actions. LeCun has a foundational paper that nobody is going to read. So, I'll summarize it as best I can. Essentially, the idea is that the current trajectory of LLM development is doomed. As long as they are predicting the next token, real understanding — the emergence of real meaning — is impossible. LeCun proposes training neural networks on physical world data, assuming that building a model of it will allow the system to discard details and focus on meaning.

I agree with LeCun that using world data will partially solve the data scarcity problem. But here I see a problem that engineers might not understand. A physical model of the world is actually much poorer than human knowledge. Newton described the entire infinite number of possible falls with a few lines of formulas. I doubt LeCun wants to spend billions of dollars on this wonderful deduction.
